metadata.task_description: Cannot reset/delete selected items in division monitor - only fetch diff works, cannot reset selected category

relevant_files.path: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/division_monitor.py
relevant_files.rationale: Primary widget containing DivisionMonitorWidget and DivisionCard - no per-category selection state exists to reset
relevant_files.topic_relation: State management exploration reveals DivisionMonitorWidget has NO selection state for categories; all categories displayed passively after fetch with no selection tracking mechanism

key_code.description: dict storing category UI widgets but NOT selection state
Updates stats but stores details as lists without any selection bitmask
Only controls visibility - no per-category selection state ever set or reset
Never creates category selection checkboxes or reset button

relevant_files.path: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/main_window.py
relevant_files.rationale: MainWindow provides example of working reset_filters() function and reset_docid table with selection model
relevant_files.topic_relation: main_window.reset_filters() shows how to reset UI state; reset_docid_table demonstrates checkbox-based selection

key_code.description: Clears all filter fields - demonstrates reset pattern for UI state
Pattern for extracting selected items from checkbox table

relevant_files.path: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/division_run_dialog.py
relevant_files.rationale: DivisionRunDialog shows category_key passed via constructor - demonstrates category-based operation state storage
relevant_files.topic_relation: category_key stored as instance attribute and used to build fetch worker and payload

key_code.description: Stores category_key as self.category_key for dialog lifecycle

missing_patterns.description: No QCheckBox, QTreeWidget selection model, or any bitmask tracking which categories are selected by user
missing_patterns.evidence: _category_widgets dict has no 'selected' key; no selectionChanged signal; no _selected_categories set

missing_patterns.description: No reset button or clear action that would clear category selection state (because none exists)
missing_patterns.evidence: Top controls only have refresh_btn, only_miss_check, use_builtin_check - no reset/clear selection button

working_patterns.description: Hide/show entire DivisionCard based on aggregate miss count - works at card-level, not category-level
working_patterns.usage: Filtering exists at division level but not per-category within a division

working_patterns.description: reset_docid_table uses QTableWidgetItem with ItemIsUserCheckable flag to track selection
working_patterns.usage: Example of checkbox-based selection storage pattern used elsewhere in app

integration_points.file: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/division_monitor.py
integration_points.description: Signal emits division_code, cat_key, cat_label, mode - NO similar signal for selection change or reset

integration_points.file: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/division_monitor.py
integration_points.description: Entry point for updating category state. No corresponding method for resetting or clearing selection state

integration_points.file: D:/Gawean Rebinmas/Browser_Auto_key_in new/Auto Key In Refactor/app/ui/division_monitor.py
integration_points.description: Stores category stats but no selection/visibility state

constraints.state_isolation: DivisionMonitorWidget state is ephemeral - no persistence of selection state between refresh cycles
constraints.no_selection_model: No QItemSelectionModel or similar mechanism for tracking multi-category selection within a division card
constraints.fetch_implicit: All categories fetched simultaneously via _refresh_all() - no lazy load per selected category

required_options: What does 'selected items' mean specifically? Options: (a) categories user clicked to highlight/filter, (b) individual rows in Details dialog, (c) cards visible via Show Only Extra, or (d) something else?
What should 'reset' do? Options: (a) clear category selection checkboxes, (b) reset all category stats to 0, (c) hide selected categories again, or (d) revert to initial no-fetch state?
clarification_needs.recommended: Also clarify: should reset apply to (a) single division or (b) all divisions at once? And should reset preserve fetched data but just clear selection, or re-fetch after reset?

next_steps: Define selection state storage mechanism: add 'selected: bool' key to _category_widgets dict entries, OR create _selected_categories: set[str] at DivisionMonitorWidget level
Add selection UI: checkboxes in category cards (L614-631 top row) or use existing detail_btn/sync_btn click as implicit selection
Add reset button to top controls (L773-806 area) that clears _selected_categories set and optionally re-applies filter visibility
Add selection_changed Signal to DivisionCard or DivisionMonitorWidget if selection should trigger other UI updates
Decide reset scope: should reset just selection state, or also re-fetch data for all categories?
